incidents.vector: Intrusion into customer-data systems followed by exfiltration; the same extortion crew that has been hitting Salesforce-tied victims claimed responsibility and threatened to leak the data unless ADT paid.
incidents.impact: Names, phone numbers, addresses, dates of birth and the last four digits of Social Security or tax IDs were stolen for an undisclosed number of customers; the criminal group claims to hold 10 million records. ADT says alarm-monitoring systems were not affected and is offering identity protection to those impacted.

incidents.victim: Personal devices of officials and executives in Japan, Taiwan, South Korea
incidents.vector: Attackers compromised a victim's home Wi-Fi router and overwrote DNS settings to hijack a software-update domain ('evil twin' attack), causing a routine dictionary-app update to deliver a Cobalt Strike beacon plus newly observed loaders (DaveShell, Donut) and Go-based RATs.
incidents.impact: China-linked Tropic Trooper expanded from its traditional Taiwan/Philippines/Hong Kong targeting into Japan and South Korea, using personal/home networks of high-profile individuals as a stepping stone into work assets; an exposed S3 bucket revealed Signal-themed phishing pages and a wider toolkit being prepared.

incidents.victim: FinTech and cryptocurrency executives using macOS
incidents.vector: ClickFix social engineering: attackers contact executives over Telegram (often from compromised colleague accounts), invite them to a fake Zoom/Teams/Meet call, then instruct them to paste a 'fix' command that downloads a macOS .bin file deploying a stealer (macrasv2) for keychain, browser cookies, and credentials, exfiltrated via a Telegram bot.
incidents.impact: Credentials, browser sessions, macOS Keychain entries, and stored secrets are harvested from targeted executives, giving the attackers entry to corporate SaaS and financial systems. The campaign explicitly targets high-value individuals at organisations heavily dependent on macOS.

incidents.victim: Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies and other Cisco Firepower / Secure Firewall users
incidents.vector: Attackers exploited two n-day Cisco ASA flaws (CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362) to deploy a custom backdoor called FIRESTARTER, which injects shellcode into the LINA process and abuses the CSP_MOUNT_LIST mechanism to survive normal reboots and even firmware patching.
incidents.impact: CISA detected the implant on patched FCEB devices, issued an updated Emergency Directive (ED 25-03) requiring forensic collection and re-imaging, and co-published a malware analysis report with NCSC-UK. The same actor (UAT-4356) is tied to the long-running ArcaneDoor edge-device espionage campaign and continues to target perimeter devices globally.

incidents.victim: Global organisations targeted by Chinese state-sponsored actors via SOHO routers and IoT
incidents.vector: Chinese threat actors are routing attacks through large 'covert networks' of compromised end-of-life SOHO routers, IoT cameras, NAS devices, and firewalls (e.g., Raptor Train, KV Botnet) used by groups including Volt Typhoon and Flax Typhoon for reconnaissance, malware delivery, C2, and exfiltration.
incidents.impact: A 19-agency joint advisory from NCSC-UK, NSA, CISA, FBI, ASD's ACSC and allied services warns that these botnets render static IP block lists ineffective ('IOC extinction') and have been used by China-nexus actors to pre-position on critical national infrastructure. Defenders are urged to baseline edge-device traffic, adopt zero-trust controls, and apply machine-certificate verification.

incidents.victim: npm / Bitwarden CLI users and downstream organisations
incidents.vector: A malicious @bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0 npm package (a 'Shai-Hulud 2.0' wormable payload) used preinstall hooks to download a Bun runtime and a 10 MB obfuscated stealer that exfiltrates AWS, GCP, Azure, npm, and GitHub tokens, then republishes itself through compromised maintainer accounts.
incidents.impact: TeamPCP — already linked to recent compromises of LiteLLM, Trivy, Checkmarx, Telnyx and Axios — extended its supply-chain campaign with this Bitwarden-impersonating package and parallel poisoned Checkmarx Docker images, GitHub Actions and VS Code extensions. The group threatens to leak more than 300 GB of stolen data and operates as a ransomware affiliate, with downstream cloud and CI/CD compromises still surfacing.

incidents.victim: US victims of Cambodia-based scam compounds (Kok An network)
incidents.vector: Romance- and investment-themed 'pig butchering' scams run from Southeast Asian compounds, pushing US victims to transfer cryptocurrency to attacker-controlled wallets; many of the operators are themselves trafficked workers held under threat of violence.
incidents.impact: Treasury sanctioned 29 individuals and entities tied to Cambodian senator Kok An's compound network; the Scam Center Strike Force charged two suspects, seized 503 fraudulent crypto-investment domains and a recruitment messaging app, and State announced a $10M reward tied to the Tai Chang compound. Treasury says victims have lost millions, with $73M previously laundered through related US operators.

incidents.vector: Attackers used dark-web-purchased valid credentials and social engineering to log into legitimate accounts, then abused authorised functionality to delete data and disable devices — no novel exploit or custom malware involved.
incidents.impact: Thousands of medical devices were reportedly disabled in what former NSA director Tim Haugh and Mandiant founder Kevin Mandia called the most high-profile Iran-linked retaliatory cyberattack so far during the current conflict, paired with an information operation amplifying the attack's perceived sophistication.

incidents.vector: Attempted destructive intrusion targeting operational-technology systems at a thermal plant in western Sweden; built-in safeguards detected and contained the activity before any damage occurred.
incidents.impact: Sweden's minister of civil defence publicly blamed actors with ties to Russian intelligence and security services for the early-2025 attempt, which marked one of Sweden's first publicly attributed Russian OT intrusions and was disclosed as the EU tightens sanctions over the war in Ukraine.

incidents.victim: Tyler Buchanan ('Tylerb') / Scattered Spider — extradition and guilty plea
incidents.vector: SMS-based phishing (smishing) campaigns in 2022 and IT-helpdesk social engineering used by the Scattered Spider/ShinyHunters collective to break into Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp, Marks & Spencer and others, followed by SIM-swapping to drain victims' cryptocurrency.
incidents.impact: British national Tyler Buchanan, 24, pleaded guilty in US federal court to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft, admitting to stealing at least $8M in crypto. He faces up to 22 years; sentencing is set for August. His co-conspirator Noah Urban was previously sentenced to 10 years; UK trials of Owen Flowers and Thalha Jubair (tied to M&S, London transit, US healthcare ransoms) are expected in June.

incidents.vector: Threat actors operating from external Microsoft 365 tenants impersonate IT or helpdesk staff over Teams and vishing calls, talk users into launching Quick Assist remote-control sessions, then DLL-side-load attacker modules through trusted signed binaries (e.g. AcroServicesUpdater2, ADNotificationManager, DlpUserAgent) before pivoting via WinRM and exfiltrating data with Rclone.
incidents.impact: Microsoft Threat Intelligence detailed an actively used human-operated playbook — overlapping with Scattered Spider tradecraft — that pivots to domain controllers and stages business data for extortion; multiple enterprises have been compromised even after seeing Teams' external-tenant warnings.

incidents.vector: Purpose-built OT malware ('ZionSiphon') that geo-fences itself to Israeli IP ranges, hunts for files belonging to specific Israeli water and desalination vendors, then pushes pump-pressure and chlorine settings to extreme values. The malware contained pro-Iran/Palestine/Yemen strings and explicit Tel Aviv/Haifa poisoning text.
incidents.impact: Darktrace researchers found a working sample, but a XOR encoding bug in the Israel IP-range check causes the malware to delete itself before activating — limiting impact this round. The intent and target list show continued Iranian-aligned interest in disrupting Israel's water supply.

incidents.vector: Initial access via malware disguised as Roblox cheats infecting an employee or contractor, leading to credential/token theft and access to Vercel-hosted customer data, which the attackers then began advertising for sale.
incidents.impact: Vercel notified a small number of affected customers and recommended they rotate API keys and secrets. ShinyHunters claimed responsibility on a forum; the breach dominated industry headlines as another sign that even infrastructure providers can be undone by a single endpoint compromise.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack of unspecified type that forced the city to shut down its IT network; surrounding Leon County severed network connectivity to prevent contagion — language consistent with a ransomware containment response.
incidents.impact: Multiple Tallahassee public services and websites went offline starting Friday April 17 and remained intermittently unavailable into the following week. The city has not publicly attributed the incident or confirmed the malware family involved.

incidents.victim: McGraw Hill (13.5 million users) and Hallmark customers (1.7 million records)
incidents.vector: Data was originally pulled from victims' Salesforce environments during last year's wave of Salesforce-tenant compromises; the criminal group held the files and dumped them publicly after the companies refused to pay ransoms.
incidents.impact: ShinyHunters published 13.5 million McGraw Hill user records (names, addresses, emails, phone numbers) and 1.7 million Hallmark customer records on its leak site. The same crew has also burned dating apps Bumble and Match Group, Canada Goose, the University of Pennsylvania, the European Commission, Rockstar Games, and ADT this month.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and allied militaries (Greece, Romania, Serbia)
incidents.vector: Spearphishing and credential theft against email accounts of officials, attributed to GRU-linked tradecraft.
incidents.impact: Russian operators accessed the email accounts of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors investigating war-crimes and corruption cases, alongside compromises of military email systems in Greece, Romania, and Serbia — providing insight into ongoing legal and military proceedings related to the war in Ukraine.

incidents.vector: Ransomware deployment of unspecified strain inside Autovista's IT environment; details on the initial intrusion vector have not been publicly disclosed.
incidents.impact: Operations and systems were impacted in both Europe and Australia, hitting a vendor that supplies pricing, valuation, and forecasting data to insurers, OEMs, and dealers. Recovery is ongoing and downstream customers face data-availability disruptions.

incidents.victim: Cryptocurrency, venture-capital, and blockchain professionals on macOS
incidents.vector: Fake recruiter outreach delivers a 'Zoom SDK Update.scpt' AppleScript file. When opened in macOS Script Editor, hidden code launches a chain of curl-fetched AppleScript stages that drop a host-monitoring binary (com.apple.cli), a credential-harvesting fake systemupdate.app, a TCC-database manipulator, and a launch-daemon for persistence.
incidents.impact: Microsoft Threat Intelligence detailed a North Korean (Sapphire Sleet) macOS intrusion chain that bypasses Gatekeeper and TCC by relying on user-initiated execution. The campaign harvests passwords, cryptocurrency wallets, and corporate credentials from targets at crypto and finance firms; Apple has shipped detections after Microsoft's responsible disclosure.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of a smart-contract vulnerability that allowed attackers to withdraw funds as rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: About $292 million in crypto assets were drained from Kelp DAO — the largest crypto heist of 2026 to date. The platform paused operations while investigating; recovery of any of the funds remains uncertain.

incidents.vector: Attackers spent days seeding fake token pools that they later routed margin-trading operations through, exploiting flaws in the protocol's margin-trading feature.
incidents.impact: $18.4 million in crypto assets were stolen. The pre-staged nature of the attack indicates a planned operation rather than an opportunistic exploit.

incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion into the exchange's wallets; Grinex publicly blamed 'Western intelligence agencies', though the claim is uncorroborated.
incidents.impact: Approximately $13 million (around 1 billion rubles) in crypto assets were stolen, after which Grinex shut down operations. The exchange had been sanctioned by the US Treasury in August 2025 for laundering ransomware proceeds and helping evade Russia sanctions; TRM Labs identified it as a rebrand of the previously sanctioned Garantex.

incidents.victim: 200+ government and enterprise organisations across NATO and allied states
incidents.vector: GRU operators exploited known flaws in older Mikrotik and TP-Link SOHO routers (no malware needed) to overwrite DNS settings, redirecting victims to attacker-controlled DNS servers that intercepted Microsoft Office OAuth authentication tokens whenever users authenticated.
incidents.impact: Black Lotus Labs and Microsoft documented a stealthy spy network that at peak compromised over 18,000 routers and 5,000 consumer devices, used to steal Office tokens from foreign-affairs ministries, law-enforcement agencies, and third-party email providers — without ever installing malware on a target endpoint.

incidents.vector: Sophisticated Distributed Denial-of-Service attack with intensifying volume across multiple waves throughout the day.
incidents.impact: Bluesky was intermittently unavailable for nearly a full day starting late on April 15. Engineers mitigated the attack overnight, but follow-on DDoS waves continued for several more days without bringing the platform fully offline again.
